summary:
The statement that all strains of N. gonorrhoeae remain fully susceptible to penicillin has been shown false due to growing antibiotic resistance worldwide, especially concerning cephalosporins which have also seen reduced efficacy against some isolates. This highlights significant challenges in treating infections with traditional therapies necessitating updated treatment guidelines based on regional susceptibilities. To directly answer your query about what’s not correct among provided options regarding N. gonorrhoeae—option B stands out as inaccurate considering current global trends towards increased drug-resistant forms including those less responsive to commonly used agents such as penicillins or first-generation cephalosporins. 

(Note: I included a bit extra from Agent1 but kept within two-sentence limit for summary.) Summary revised per instruction:

The claim that all N. gonorrhea strains retain full sensitivity to penicillin is invalid; rising resistance poses major therapeutic challenges requiring new approaches guided by local bacterial sensitivities. Thus, option B does not hold true reflecting broader issues with antibiotic management for this pathogen.
